what: Structure and Reproducibility Checks for Student Assignment Repositories
title: Structure and Reproducibility Checks for Student Assignment Repositories
text: checklist provides tools instructors automatically check structure reproducibility student assignment submissions. goal check assignment correctness, rather submission complete, well-formed, reproducible. package includes checks : required, allowed, disallowed files required, allowed, disallowed package dependencies successful rendering R Markdown Quarto documents Checks can run locally , usefully, automatically student submission using GitHub Actions similar continuous integration service.

title: Structure and Reproducibility Checks for Student Assignment Repositories
text: can install development version package GitHub :

title: Structure and Reproducibility Checks for Student Assignment Repositories
text: Let’s look simple example type assignment student might turn . files available inst/examples/hw1 within repository. already installed package directory can also found using system.file("examples/hw1", package="checklist"). can now use checklist express simple checks files directory. example, wanted make sure students submit rendered version homework use following check: Alternatively, may want prevent students turning rendered version (check reproducibility work) can done explicitly may instead want explicit files allowed, ensuring students added renamed anything: default package ignores hidden files (files whose name starts .). can included checks using = TRUE argument. refine , may want allow .gitignore well .Rproj.user/ folder. can added files argument, standard glob wildcards supported make life easier:
code: library(checklist) dir = system.file("examples/hw1", package="checklist")  # Show the project contents fs::dir_tree("inst/examples/hw1") #> inst/examples/hw1 #> ├── README.md #> ├── hw1.Rproj #> └── hw1.qmd check_required_files("hw1.md", dir) #> The following required files are missing: #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ✖ hw1.md check_disallowed_files("hw1.md", dir) check_allowed_files(c("README.md", "hw1.qmd", "hw1.Rproj"), dir) check_allowed_files(c("README.md", "hw1.qmd", "hw1.Rproj"), dir, all = TRUE) #> Disallowed files found: (please remove the following files) #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ✖ .hidden check_allowed_files(   c("README.md", "hw1.qmd", "hw1.Rproj", ".gitignore", ".Rproj.user/*"),   dir, all = TRUE ) #> Disallowed files found: (please remove the following files) #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ✖ .hidden

title: Structure and Reproducibility Checks for Student Assignment Repositories
text: checks useful run automatically student submissions, example via GitHub Actions workflow student’s repository. quit_on_failure() ensures failed check also fails workflow run. example workflow hw1 assignment ships package templates/check_assignment.yml, can located system.file("templates/check_assignment.yml", package = "checklist"). can copied assignment repository .github/workflows/check_assignment.yml:
code: on: push name: Check Assignment jobs:   check-allowed-files:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest     container:       image: rocker/r2u:latest     steps:     - name: Checkout       uses: actions/checkout@v7     - name: Install checklist       run: |         installGithub.r rundel/checklist       shell: bash     - name: Check Files       run: |         checklist::quit_on_failure({           checklist::check_allowed_files(c("hw1.qmd", "hw1.Rproj", "README.md"))         })       shell: Rscript {0}   check-renders:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest     container:       image: rocker/r2u:latest     steps:     - name: Checkout       uses: actions/checkout@v7     - name: Install quarto and checklist       run: |         apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends wget         wget -q https://github.com/quarto-dev/quarto-cli/releases/download/v1.8.27/quarto-1.8.27-linux-amd64.deb         apt-get install -y ./quarto-1.8.27-linux-amd64.deb && rm quarto-*.deb         installGithub.r rundel/checklist       shell: bash     - name: Check Renders       run: |         checklist::check_qmd_renders("hw1.qmd", install_missing = TRUE)       shell: Rscript {0}

what: Check for allowed or disallowed files in a project or directory — check_files
title: Check for allowed or disallowed files in a project or directory — check_files
text: Check allowed disallowed files project directory

title: Check for allowed or disallowed files in a project or directory — check_files
code: find_files(   files,   dir = here::here(),   all = FALSE,   recurse = TRUE,   type = c("file", "directory", "any"),   regex = FALSE,   invert = FALSE )  check_allowed_files(   files,   dir = here::here(),   all = FALSE,   recurse = TRUE,   type = c("file", "directory", "any"),   regex = FALSE )  check_disallowed_files(files, dir = here::here(), regex = FALSE)  check_required_files(files, dir = here::here())

title: Check for allowed or disallowed files in a project or directory — check_files
text: files Character vector file names patterns match (wildcards default, regular expressions regex = TRUE). check_required_files() matches file names literally support patterns. dir Directory check TRUE include hidden files recurse TRUE recurse fully, positive number number levels recurse type File type return, one "file", "directory", "" regex TRUE use files regular expressions otherwise assume wildcard (glob) patterns invert TRUE return files match

title: Check for allowed or disallowed files in a project or directory — check_files
text: find_files() returns character vector matching file paths. check_* functions return TRUE FALSE invisibly, indicating whether check passed.

title: Check for allowed or disallowed files in a project or directory — check_files
text: find_files(): Find files match given pattern (glob regex) within given directory check_allowed_files(): Check allowed file(s) exist check_disallowed_files(): Check disallowed file(s) exist check_required_files(): Check required file(s) exist, file names matched literally (patterns supported)

title: Check for allowed or disallowed files in a project or directory — check_files
code: dir = system.file("examples/hw1", package = "checklist")  find_files("*.qmd", dir) #> hw1.qmd  check_allowed_files(c("README.md", "hw1.qmd", "hw1.Rproj"), dir) check_disallowed_files("hw1.md", dir) check_required_files("hw1.pdf", dir) #> The following required files are missing: #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ✖ hw1.pdf

title: Check for allowed or disallowed package dependencies within a project or directory using pak::scan_deps(). — check_pkgs
code: installed_pkgs()  missing_pkgs(   dir = here::here(),   regexp = "[.](R|r|Rmd|rmd|Rnw|rnw|Qmd|qmd)$",   recurse = TRUE )  install_missing_pkgs(   dir = here::here(),   regexp = "[.](R|r|Rmd|rmd|Rnw|rnw|Qmd|qmd)$",   recurse = TRUE,   ... )  find_pkgs(   dir = here::here(),   regexp = "[.](R|r|Rmd|rmd|Rnw|rnw|Qmd|qmd)$",   full = FALSE,   recurse = TRUE )  check_allowed_pkgs(   pkgs,   dir = here::here(),   regexp = "[.](R|r|Rmd|rmd|Rnw|rnw|Qmd|qmd)$",   recurse = TRUE )  check_disallowed_pkgs(   pkgs,   dir = here::here(),   regexp = "[.](R|r|Rmd|rmd|Rnw|rnw|Qmd|qmd)$",   recurse = TRUE )  check_required_pkgs(   pkgs,   dir = here::here(),   regexp = "[.](R|r|Rmd|rmd|Rnw|rnw|Qmd|qmd)$",   recurse = TRUE )

title: Check for allowed or disallowed package dependencies within a project or directory using pak::scan_deps(). — check_pkgs
text: dir Directory search. regexp Regular expression used select files, defaults matching R, Rmd, Rnw, qmd files (case variants). recurse directory recursively explored (.e. match files sub directories) ... Additional arguments passed install.packages(). full full data frame dependencies returned just vector package names. pkgs Character vector package names

title: Check for allowed or disallowed package dependencies within a project or directory using pak::scan_deps(). — check_pkgs
text: installed_pkgs(), missing_pkgs(), find_pkgs() return character vector package names (find_pkgs() returns data frame dependency details full = TRUE). install_missing_pkgs() called side effect installing packages returns NULL invisibly. check_* functions return TRUE FALSE invisibly, indicating whether check passed.

title: Check for allowed or disallowed package dependencies within a project or directory using pak::scan_deps(). — check_pkgs
text: installed_pkgs(): Returns vector installed packages. missing_pkgs(): Returns vector packages found find_pkgs currently installed. install_missing_pkgs(): Installs missing packages found missing_pkgs. find_pkgs(): Find packages used within project using pak::scan_deps(). check_allowed_pkgs(): Check allowed packages used check_disallowed_pkgs(): Check disallowed packages used check_required_pkgs(): Check required packages used

title: Check for allowed or disallowed package dependencies within a project or directory using pak::scan_deps(). — check_pkgs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ dir = system.file("examples/package", package = "checklist")  find_pkgs(dir) missing_pkgs(dir)  check_allowed_pkgs(c("dplyr", "ggplot2"), dir) check_disallowed_pkgs("nlme", dir) check_required_pkgs("dplyr", dir) } # }

Handle runtime errors — handle_error
text: Evaluates given expression runs on_success, on_error, on_warning expressions depending result initial evaluation.

title: Handle runtime errors — handle_error

title: Handle runtime errors — handle_error
text: expr Expression evaluate on_success Expression evaluate expr succeeds on_error Expression evaluate expr generates error on_warning Expression evaluate expr generates warning finally Expression evaluate expr on_success on_error on_warning, usually used cleanup.

title: Handle runtime errors — handle_error
text: Invisible result expr succeeds, otherwise error warning object.

title: Handle runtime errors — handle_error
code: handle_error(   1 + 1,   on_success = message("success"),   on_error = message("error") ) #> success  handle_error(   stop("Something went wrong"),   on_success = message("success"),   on_error = message("error") ) #> error

title: Quit on Failure — quit_on_failure
text: value expr, invisibly. element result TRUE R session terminated exit status 1.

title: Quit on Failure — quit_on_failure
text: needed using check_* functions within continuous integration pipeline signal step failed.

title: Quit on Failure — quit_on_failure
checklist 0.2.0
text: example assignments inst/examples/ now use Quarto (qmd) documents instead R Markdown, example GitHub Actions workflow included inst/templates/. Renamed glob argument regexp find_pkgs(), missing_pkgs(), install_missing_pkgs(), check_*_pkgs() functions; file selection now uses regular expression. Removed unused full argument check_allowed_pkgs(), check_disallowed_pkgs(), check_required_pkgs(). Fixed quit_on_failure() logical vector TRUE values longer exits error status. find_files() now treats empty files vector matching nothing (everything invert = TRUE), check_disallowed_files() patterns passes instead reporting every file.

title: checklist 0.1.1
text: Added handle_error() help manage results check_rmd_renders() check_qmd_renders(). Switch using renv pak scanning files dependencies.

title: checklist 0.1.0
text: Initial version checklist.
